limitations: Operator chat timing inside this slice is approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
The 0.6.19 package report proves build inventory only; it records distributed:false and does not establish live prerelease authority.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator reported that presets still initialized unreliably until plugin reload, a saved image-directory preset could still auto-play images across multiple instances, movies still reset to the beginning on app switch, the hosted gradient sometimes required a plugin reload to appear, and the remaining defaults should stop carrying shuffle and loop as on-by-default behavior.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: The code review isolated several overlapping seams: preset application still rebuilt playlists from saved media paths, hosted load did not honor explicit playlist arguments, file selections were still inflating into sibling playlists, focus-loss handling did not snapshot playback time before the focus generation changed, still-image loads could leave the placeholder grid until a later refresh, and hosted startup allowed too short a retry window.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.19 cleanly after correcting the new focus-loss snapshot helper to use the actual timeline-reader signature.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Regenerated the 0.6.19 package inventory with -SkipVarDistribute and confirmed the report shows distributed:false, so the package exists only as build inventory and not as a live prerelease authority.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Redeployed the raw modern_tv hosted-player proof as the canonical 0.6.19 alpha dev_deploy seam after the preset and playlist hardening changes.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Refreshed the baseline direct-player raw asset to the same 0.6.19 alpha core and removed the generic live dev_cua_player/dev_plugin_player copies so one raw authority remained for this version.

decisions: 0.6.19 is its own semantic version because preset, playlist, focus, and hosted-startup behavior changed after 0.6.18 had already been built for testing.
The current live authority seam for this slice remains raw dev_deploy Custom artifacts, not the .var package.
Loop mode now defaults to none and shuffle now defaults to off for new player records instead of keeping older dev-convenience defaults.
Explicit file loads should stay single-item by default; only an explicit playlist argument or a directory selection should widen the playlist.
Focus-loss recovery should snapshot current playback state before the focus generation changes rather than trying to infer resume state later.
This single-file playlist policy later proved to be the wrong rule for the operator's core-player workflow and should have been rolled back with the rest of the drift-period compiled behavior.
openQuestions: Does 0.6.19 stop the app-switch reset-to-beginning seam for the problematic movie tests?
Does 0.6.19 fully stop saved image-directory presets from reviving autoplay or stale grid overlays without a plugin reload?
What is the correct mixed-media playlist policy when the same playlist intentionally contains both images and videos?
notes: The 0.6.19 package report exists because the hosted proof script expects package inventory, and the final report records distributed:false so the live authority did not drift away from raw dev_deploy.
This slice intentionally captures the mixed image-and-video playlist issue as future truth without overcommitting to a final mixed-media policy in the same packet.

The later recovery lane drifted by preserving this compiled single-file behavior as if it were harmless context. The correct rollback rule is that any operator-visible behavior policy changed during a high-drift period must be treated as rollback candidate code.
